title: Production Hardening — SeaweedFS Erasure Coding and Volume Server Scaling
subtasks: Configure SeaweedFS erasure coding (EC) on the cto-pay-receipts bucket to provide data durability beyond simple replication — set EC policy (e.g., 10+4 or 3+2 depending on cluster size)
Scale SeaweedFS volume servers from current count to minimum 3 volume server replicas for redundancy
Configure SeaweedFS replication policy for the cto-pay-receipts bucket (e.g., 010 for rack-level replication or 001 for same-rack)
Verify erasure coding and replication by uploading a test receipt object, stopping a volume server, and confirming read availability
Create a SeaweedFS backup CronJob that periodically snapshots the cto-pay-receipts bucket metadata to a secondary storage location
Update the cto-pay-infra-endpoints ConfigMap if any SeaweedFS endpoint changes due to scaling

title: Production Hardening — TLS Certificates via cert-manager and Internal CA
subtasks: Verify cert-manager is installed in the cluster (check for cert-manager CRDs — gitlab/gitlab-certmanager service exists, confirm it serves the CTO namespace or deploy a ClusterIssuer)
Create a ClusterIssuer or Issuer resource for Let's Encrypt (production and staging) in the cto namespace
Create Certificate resources for all cto-pay externally-facing services (cto-web, cto-controller endpoints) with automatic renewal
Configure an internal CA Issuer for service-to-service mTLS within the cto namespace (SeaweedFS S3 API, internal controller endpoints)
Issue internal TLS certificates for SeaweedFS S3 endpoint and update the cto-pay-infra-endpoints ConfigMap to use HTTPS URLs where applicable
Verify certificate issuance: kubectl get certificates -n cto shows Ready status, check expiry dates, confirm auto-renewal triggers

title: Production Hardening — Ingress Controller Configuration for CTO Pay Services
subtasks: Determine the existing ingress controller in the cluster (check for NGINX Ingress, Envoy/Cilium Ingress, or Cloudflare Tunnel — cloudflare-operator-system namespace exists with CRDs)
Create Ingress resources for cto-pay controller service with TLS termination using cert-manager certificates from Task 9
Configure rate limiting on the ingress for cto-pay API endpoints (prevent abuse of settlement/deposit endpoints)
Set up path-based routing: /api/* to cto-controller, /health to health check endpoint
Configure appropriate timeouts, request size limits, and CORS headers for the Solana RPC proxy pattern
If using Cloudflare Tunnel (AccessTunnel/ClusterTunnel CRDs detected): create a ClusterTunnel or AccessTunnel resource for cto-pay with appropriate access policies

title: Production Hardening — Cloudflare CDN Configuration for Static Assets
subtasks: Configure Cloudflare DNS records for the cto-pay domain (proxied through Cloudflare for CDN and DDoS protection)
Set up Cloudflare page rules or cache rules for static assets served by cto-web (JS bundles, CSS, images — cache TTL 1 year with content hashing)
Configure Cloudflare WAF rules to protect cto-pay API endpoints (rate limiting, bot protection, known attack pattern blocking)
Enable Cloudflare SSL/TLS in Full (Strict) mode to ensure end-to-end encryption between Cloudflare and the origin
Configure Cloudflare firewall rules to restrict access to admin/operator endpoints by IP allowlist if applicable
Verify CDN caching: confirm static assets return cf-cache-status: HIT headers and API requests return cf-cache-status: DYNAMIC

title: Production Hardening — Network Policies Restricting Pod-to-Pod Traffic
subtasks: Leverage Cilium network policy CRDs (CiliumNetworkPolicy detected in cluster) to create namespace-level default-deny ingress and egress policies for the cto namespace
Create CiliumNetworkPolicy allowing cto-controller pods to communicate with SeaweedFS S3 endpoint (port 8333) within the cto namespace
Create CiliumNetworkPolicy allowing cto-controller pods to make egress connections to Solana RPC endpoints (Helius devnet/mainnet URLs on port 443)
Create CiliumNetworkPolicy allowing cto-controller pods to communicate with Qdrant (if used for receipt indexing) on its service port
Create CiliumNetworkPolicy allowing ingress from the ingress controller namespace to cto-controller service port
Create CiliumNetworkPolicy allowing cto namespace pods to reach external-secrets webhook and OpenBao for secret synchronization
Test network policies: verify a test pod in the cto namespace cannot reach services it should not (e.g., gitlab namespace), and can reach services it should (SeaweedFS, external RPC)
Document all network policy rules in infra/gitops/network-policies/ directory with YAML manifests and a README explaining the traffic matrix

title: Production Hardening — Observability Stack: OpenTelemetry Collector, Tracing, and Alerting
subtasks: Deploy an OpenTelemetry Collector in the cto namespace configured to receive traces, metrics, and logs from cto-pay controller pods
Configure OpenTelemetry sampling rates: 100% for errors, 10% for successful settlement transactions, 1% for health checks — tune based on expected volume
Configure the OpenTelemetry Collector to export metrics to Datadog (datadog-agent service detected in datadog-agent namespace) using the Datadog exporter
Configure the OpenTelemetry Collector to export traces to Datadog APM (or deploy Jaeger with persistent storage if Datadog tracing is not available)
Create Datadog monitor alert rules for SLO violations: settlement success rate < 99.5%, p99 settlement latency > 5s, vault balance discrepancy, program pause events
Create Datadog monitor for infrastructure health: SeaweedFS volume server availability, ExternalSecret sync failures, certificate expiry within 14 days
Instrument the cto-pay controller application code with OpenTelemetry SDK spans for key operations: deposit processing, settlement execution, receipt storage, RPC calls
Create a Datadog dashboard for cto-pay operational visibility: settlement volume, deposit/withdraw trends, daily cap utilization, error rates by type
Configure log aggregation: ensure cto-controller pod logs are collected by the Datadog agent with appropriate tags (service:cto-pay, env:production)
